phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 109.4 dBA, gradually settling to 77.4-84.4 dBA midway through session. Speech event detected at 55s mark with confidence 0.72. Very loud environment detected; concentration significantly compromised.

phase_1.gps_motion: GPS positioning shows initial variance of 5.3m, refining to 1.4m accuracy over session duration. Accelerometer readings show user quickly becoming immobile and remaining seated throughout.

phase_2.correlations: The shift toward sustained stillness aligns with GPS signal stabilization. Audio channel demonstrates notable transitions.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session exemplifies near-optimal conditions: minimal acoustic disturbance (84.4 dB), stable positioning, and strong focus indicators. Duration (60s) borderline for sustained concentration assessment.

phase_3.recommendations: Invest in noise-canceling technology to mitigate high ambient levels. Gradually increase session length from current 60s to develop deeper concentration habits.

reason: Score weighted toward audio environment (84.4dB—loud), stable positioning, and session duration (60s). Overall favorable conditions.
